parameters.content:
# Document Ingestion & OCR Processing

## What happens here
Each presentation file is downloaded from Google Drive and processed 
through Mistral OCR, which extracts both text and images from every slide.

## Why this matters
Standard text extraction misses visual content. By using multimodal OCR, 
every slide is fully captured — including charts, diagrams, and images — 
ensuring no information is lost before embedding.

## Output
One item per slide, containing:
### Text
Cleaned markdown content extracted from the slide
### Images
Base64-encoded images with metadata, stored separately in Supabase Storage

# Text Processing & Embedding

## What happens here
Slide markdown is cleaned and normalized before being converted 
into vector embeddings using OpenAI's embedding model.

## Why this matters
Raw OCR output contains noise — escape characters, image references, 
and formatting artifacts that reduce embedding quality. Clean text 
produces more accurate semantic search results.

## Output
One embedding vector per slide, stored in Supabase pgvector alongside:
### Text content
Cleaned and normalized slide text
### Metadata
Document title, slide number, image count, and extracted links

# Image Processing & Storage

## What happens here
Images extracted from each slide are split into individual items, 
uploaded to Supabase Storage, and a permanent public URL is generated 
for each image.

## Why this matters
Storing images separately from text allows the AI agent to include 
visual references directly in its responses — giving users access 
to the original slide visuals when relevant.

## Output
One database row per image, containing:
### Image URL
Permanent public link to the image in Supabase Storage
### Metadata
Document title, slide number, and image ID linking back to the parent slide

# Vision AI & Image Embedding

## What happens here
Each image is individually analyzed by Mistral Pixtral vision model, 
which generates a concise semantic description optimized for search retrieval.
Descriptions are then embedded using OpenAI's embedding model.

## Why this matters
Images cannot be searched directly — by generating text descriptions, 
visual content becomes semantically searchable alongside slide text. 
A user asking about a graph or chart can retrieve the relevant image 
even without knowing it exists.

## Output
One embedding vector per image, stored in Supabase pgvector alongside:
### Description
Concise semantic summary of the image content
### Metadata
Document title, slide number, and image ID

# Document Metadata Enrichment
## Topic and summary for every document is created as a context for AI Agent that retrieves the embeddings from supabase

## What happens here

Before slides are stored in the vector database, each presentation 
is automatically analyzed to extract a topic and summary.

## How it works:
1. The first 3 slides of each presentation are sampled as a 
   representative overview
2. An LLM generates a topic label and summary based on the content
3. This metadata is attached to every slide from that presentation

## Why this matters:
When a user asks a question, the AI agent has both slide-level 
detail and document-level context — improving answer quality 
and relevance significantly.

# Image Metadata Merge

## What happens here
Vision AI descriptions and their embeddings are merged with the 
corresponding image metadata — including the Supabase Storage URL, 
slide number, and document information.

## Why this matters
Embeddings alone are not useful without context. By linking each 
vector to its source image URL and slide metadata, the retrieval 
system can return not just relevant text but direct visual references 
with full traceability back to the original presentation.

## Output
One complete database row per image, ready for Supabase insertion:
### Embedding
Vector representation of the image description
### Image URL
Direct link to the image in Supabase Storage
### Metadata
Document title, slide number, document topic, and document summary

values.content:
=You will receive slide content from a presentation. Extract the subject and summary.

You MUST return your response in EXACTLY this format, no exceptions:

document_id: {{ $json.document_id }}
document_title: {{ $json.document_title }}

**Topic:** [one line description]

**Summary:** [2-3 sentence summary]

Do not add any other text, headers, or formatting. Do not use bold for document_id or document_title.You are a document and text analyzing specialist. You analyze text content and are able to categorize the subject clearly and concisely. You keep the semantic value in your answers so that retrieving data from a vector database becomes accurate.

assignments.name: system_prompt
assignments.value: For each image assess complexity. SIMPLE means icons logos symbols photos of people decorative images or illustrations without data and requires maximum 1 sentence. COMPLEX means graphs charts tables data visualizations or technical diagrams and requires 3-5 sentences covering data trends key values axes and main insights only. Never describe aesthetics colors or symbolic meaning. Focus only on informational content relevant for educational search retrieval.

assignments.name: user_prompt
assignments.value: Analyze this image and create a concise summary optimized for semantic search retrieval. For graphs, charts, and tables: identify the visualization type, name the key variables or axes, and describe the main trend or insight the data shows. For all other images: one sentence describing what it is.

parameters.content:
# Slide Metadata Merge

## What happens here
Cleaned slide text and embeddings are merged with document-level 
metadata — topic and summary generated by the LLM in the enrichment phase.

## Why this matters
Without this merge, each slide would be stored with text content only. 
By attaching document-level context to every slide, the AI agent can 
answer questions with both slide-specific detail and a broader 
understanding of where that information comes from.

## Output
One complete database row per slide, ready for Supabase insertion:
### Embedding
Vector representation of the cleaned slide text
### Slide content
Cleaned and normalized markdown text
### Document context
Topic label and summary inherited from the parent presentation

# Vector Storage — Supabase Insert

## What happens here
Each fully enriched slide item is inserted as a row into the 
Supabase slide_rag table, including the embedding vector, 
text content, image metadata, and document context.

## Why this matters
This is the final step of the ingestion pipeline. Once inserted, 
slides become immediately searchable via pgvector cosine similarity — 
making all processed content available to the retrieval agent.

## Output
One permanent row per slide in the slide_rag table, containing:
### Embedding
1536-dimension vector for semantic similarity search
### Content
Cleaned slide text and image URLs
### Metadata
Document title, topic, summary, slide number, and content type

# RAG Ingestion Pipeline — Presentation Knowledge Base

## Purpose
This workflow processes presentation files from Google Drive into a 
fully searchable multimodal knowledge base. Both text and images are 
extracted, analyzed, and stored as vector embeddings in Supabase — 
making every slide retrievable through semantic search.

## Why multimodal matters
Most RAG pipelines index text only. This pipeline captures the full 
content of each slide — including charts, diagrams, and images — 
by combining OCR, vision AI, and embedding models. This means users 
can retrieve relevant visuals alongside text when querying the knowledge base.

## Pipeline overview
1. Files are fetched from Google Drive and processed through Mistral OCR
2. Document metadata (topic + summary) is generated by an LLM
3. Slide text is cleaned, embedded, and enriched with document context
4. Images are extracted, described by vision AI, and embedded separately
5. All vectors and image files are stored in Supabase

## Extensibility
This pipeline is built for presentations but the architecture supports 
any document type. Switching to PDFs, Word documents, or web pages 
requires only changes to the ingestion and chunking logic — 
the embedding, storage, and retrieval layers remain unchanged.

## Stack
- **OCR:** Mistral OCR (text + image extraction)
- **Vision AI:** Mistral Pixtral (image descriptions)
- **Embeddings:** OpenAI (text + images)
- **Vector store:** Supabase pgvector
- **Image storage:** Supabase Storage bucket
- **Orchestration:** n8n
